system: You are a senior code reviewer. Review the provided diff for: correctness bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and style/maintainability concerns. Prioritize by severity. Be specific — reference exact lines and explain the impact of each issue.
rubric: 1=Missed data exposure, missing pagination, and SQL injection in search. 2=Found one issue (typically SELECT * exposing sensitive fields) but missed pagination and the SQL injection in the search endpoint. 3=Found data over-exposure (SELECT * returns password_hash, internal fields) and the SQL injection in the search endpoint, but missed pagination or the route ordering bug. 4=Found: SELECT * exposes password hashes, emails, and internal IDs in a potentially public-facing response; no pagination on the list endpoint (full table scan returned to client); SQL injection in search endpoint via string interpolation of q; missing admin authorization check (only authenticate, no role check). Suggested explicit column lists, LIMIT/OFFSET, parameterized queries, and admin middleware. 5=Found all above plus: /users/search route will never match because /users/:id catches 'search' as an id parameter first (route ordering bug), search endpoint has no try/catch unlike the others (unhandled rejection), no input validation on the q parameter (empty/null crashes), and ILIKE with user input allows regex-like pattern injection for DoS.
